title: ticket-lifecycle
description: The §7.1 ticket lifecycle transition table as typed data. This policy is the human-readable DECLARATION of the machine that lifecycle.py's LIFECYCLE dict enforces in code — the two must agree; the kernel's LIFECYCLE is the executable source of truth, this is its typed mirror for review and routing. Computation routes to code: the deterministic part (is a transition legal, does its readiness gate pass) is lifecycle.py, never inference. The model's judgment lives in FORMING a ticket (triage, decomposition), never in deciding whether a transition is legal. Every transition terminates in a ledger event; single-writer discipline (REQ-CORPUS-003) means one authority (the server, or a human via the API) applies each.
single_writer: All coordination-state mutations pass through the server (REQ-CORPUS-003). Workers never write ticket state. The dispatcher sets `claimed`; the validation path drives `in-review -> done`. This designs out the claim race (§7.2): no two workers can claim one ticket because workers do not claim — the server assigns.

transitions.predicate: schema valid; target_cell exists; target_transition legal in the maturity machine; acceptance bound to a VALIDATED rubric cell; budget set (iterations+tokens); dependencies declared (use {} for none)
transitions.violation: deny; ticket stays draft with the failing predicate named

transitions.predicate: dependency cells all validated; dependency tickets done; a concurrency slot free; autonomy tier permits unattended dispatch of this transition; target_cell not blocked; worktree provisioned
transitions.violation: deny; ticket stays active until ready

transitions.predicate: artifact written to the instance; worker exited 0

for a signal-bearing transition (to validated/operating/regenerating): a signals/{cell}/... artifact exists, written by the CRITIC not the worker, AND the target cell reached the ticket's to-maturity. THE MORPHISM: ticket done <=> cell advanced, through this same gate. An authoring transition (to defined/instantiated) is applied by the server with no critic signal.
transitions.violation: deny; ticket returns to in-progress with the critic's evidence as feedback (attempts++) — never done by assertion

transitions.predicate: feedback injected as additional context; attempt counter ++

transitions.trigger: budget exhausted / no-progress signature / dependency regressed / a gate denied
transitions.predicate: reason + diagnostic written; the target cell is NOT advanced; surfaced to the compass
transitions.violation: n/a (a stop, not a transition to deny)

transitions.trigger: dependency validated, budget extended, or human unblock
transitions.predicate: the original ready-gate is re-checked from scratch
transitions.violation: deny; stays blocked until the gate passes

transitions.predicate: lease frozen; worker signalled to checkpoint and exit; orthogonal to the main path
worktree torn down; ledgered with rationale; soft (recorded, not erased)

note: Workers in ticket-orchestration are deny-on-write to coordination STATE — they never write ticket state at all (single-writer). The triager/planner/arbiter PROPOSE documents; the server applies the gated transition and appends the ledger event. The `done` morphism passes through gate-signal so the coordination board cannot disagree with the lattice (§4).
